seoTitle: How to Create an Invoice From an Estimate in QuickBooks
metaDescription: Convert an approved estimate into an invoice in QuickBooks Online to reduce manual entry and get paid faster for client projects.
intro: Turning an approved estimate directly into an invoice saves time and reduces the risk of manual entry errors. This guide walks through converting an estimate to an invoice in QuickBooks Online, a workflow especially useful for freelancers and businesses that quote projects before billing clients.

steps.heading: Open the approved estimate
steps.body: Find the estimate that's been approved by your client in QuickBooks Online.

steps.heading: Convert it into an invoice
steps.body: Use QuickBooks' built-in option to turn the estimate directly into an invoice, carrying over the line items and pricing.

steps.heading: Review the invoice details
steps.body: Check that all quantities, prices, and client information transferred correctly before sending.

steps.heading: Make any final adjustments
steps.body: Add or adjust line items if the final scope of work differs slightly from the original estimate.

steps.heading: Send the invoice to your client
steps.body: Once confirmed, send the invoice so your client can review and process payment.

faq.question: Does converting an estimate to an invoice keep the original estimate?
faq.answer: Yes, QuickBooks Online keeps the original estimate on file while creating a linked invoice from it.

faq.question: Can I edit the invoice after converting it from an estimate?
faq.answer: Yes, you can adjust line items, quantities, or pricing on the invoice if the final work differs from the estimate.

faq.question: Why convert an estimate instead of creating a new invoice?
faq.answer: It reduces manual data entry and keeps a clear link between the quoted work and the final bill, which helps with accuracy.

faq.question: Is this feature useful for businesses that don't send estimates often?
faq.answer: It's most valuable for businesses managing multiple client projects with quoted pricing, but any business using estimates can benefit from the time savings.
